scene.name: Synthetic lower-delta AOI
scene.location: Modelled on a Rhine-Meuse polder setting, Netherlands (not a real coordinate)

real_components.what: EU Floods Directive scenario definitions, return-period probability classes, six-year cycle and flood-map obligations
real_components.source: Directive 2007/60/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 23 October 2007 on the assessment and management of flood risks

real_components.what: Copernicus DEM (GLO-30) cited as the open European reference elevation surface for flood-extent modelling
real_components.source: Copernicus DEM, European Space Agency / Copernicus Programme

real_components.what: Copernicus Emergency Management Service (CEMS) and the European Flood Awareness System (EFAS) cited as the operational EU flood mapping/forecast services
real_components.source: Copernicus Emergency Management Service, European Commission JRC
real_components.attribution: © European Union, Copernicus Emergency Management Service

real_components.what: JRC Global Surface Water cited as the source for seeding permanent water bodies
real_components.source: Pekel, J.-F. et al. (2016) High-resolution mapping of global surface water and its long-term changes, Nature 540, 418-422
real_components.attribution: EC JRC / Google, Global Surface Water dataset

real_components.what: EU-Hydro river network and OpenStreetMap infrastructure cited as the connectivity and exposure references
real_components.attribution: © OpenStreetMap contributors; Copernicus Land Monitoring Service

synthetic_components.what: AOI terrain / DEM, carved river channel, coastal depression, and hillshade
synthetic_components.method: Multi-octave value-noise DEM with regional slope and channel carving, normalised to -1.5..12.5 m

synthetic_components.what: Road network, building footprints (~600 points across 5 settlements) and 5 critical assets
synthetic_components.method: Parameterised settlement clustering and polyline routing

Connectivity-aware planar inundation: a planar flood surface combined with a 4-neighbour flood-fill from permanent-water seed cells (bathtub + hydrological connectivity). First-order screening method.

notes: The AOI is entirely synthetic and reproducible from fixed seeds; it is NOT a real place and must not be read as a real flood hazard map.

The inundation model is a connectivity-aware planar (bathtub) screening method. It captures hydrological connectivity but ignores flow dynamics, hydraulic roughness, defence breaching, infiltration and timing. It is not a hydrodynamic (e.g. LISFLOOD/HEC-RAS) simulation.
Return-period to water-level mappings (30 yr -> 1.7 m, 100 yr -> 2.6 m, 300 yr -> 3.6 m) are illustrative defaults for the synthetic AOI, not calibrated stage-frequency values.
Exposure counts (buildings, road km, residents, critical assets) are computed over the synthetic infrastructure, not real cadastre. Residents use a flat 2.3 occupants/building proxy.
For statutory flood hazard and risk maps, use Member-State reporting via the EEA Flood Risk Areas Viewer and WISE-Freshwater.
